creator: crb@ChrisBowman.com
text:
copying a couple of repos to /tmp on an ZFS SSD reliably reproduces and wedges my system with:

nvme0: RECOVERY_START 6472636043166 vs 6472510248633
nvme0: RECOVERY_START 6474563842212 vs 6472510248633

nvme0: Resetting controller due to a timeout and possible hot unplug.

nvme0: resetting controller

nvme0: ABORTED - BY REQUEST (00/07) sqid:10 cid:125 cdw0:0
nvme0: failing queued i/o
nvme0: WRITE sqid:14 cid:0 nsid:1 lba:738207824 len:8
nvme0: ABORTED - BY REQUEST (00/07) sqid:14 cid:0 cdw0:0
nvme0: failing outstanding i/o

nvme0: ABORTED - BY REQUEST (00/07) sqid:14 cid:125 cdw0:0
nvme0: failing outstanding i/o

nvd0: detached

nvme0: ABORTED - BY REQUEST (00/07) sqid:7 cid:0 cdw0:0
nvme0: waiting
Solaris: WARNING: Pool 'zroot' has encountered an uncorrectable I/O failure and has been suspended.

nvme0: waiting
Solaris: WARNING: Pool 'zroot' has encountered an uncorrectable I/O failure and has been suspended.

FreeBSD 13.1 RELEASE
AMD 5950 processors
ASUS TUF Gaming PRO x570 motherboard
Currently on a WD 850 SSD with a heat sync but similar happened on 2 previous SSDs of other brands
